title: CoralNet Crawler (ReefNet image/annotation scraping toolkit)
abstract: A Python utility toolkit ('CoralNet Crawler') developed as part of the ReefNet project to scrape source metadata, point annotations, and images from CoralNet (coralnet.ucsd.edu), a community coral-reef photo-annotation platform. This is code/tooling used to construct the ReefNet dataset, not a dataset itself -- no scraped image or annotation output is present in this folder (only the scripts and an input sources list). Catalogued here for provenance completeness, linking to the reefnet-coral-image-dataset record it helped produce.

variables.name: Source
variables.label: CoralNet Source Name
variables.description: Name of a CoralNet source to scrape.

variables.name: Valid
variables.label: Include Flag
variables.description: 'yes'/'no' flag controlling whether the source is processed by the scraper.

access.how_to_request: Public GitHub repository: https://github.com/ReefNet-Project/coralnet_crawler. Requires a CoralNet account/credentials to actually run the scraper against CoralNet.

provenance.citation: Battach, Y. et al. ReefNet: A Large-scale, Taxonomically Enriched Dataset and Benchmark for Coral Reef Classification. arXiv preprint, 2025.

known_issues: This is a code/tooling repository, not a dataset -- it is included in the catalog as a provenance record for how the reefnet-coral-image-dataset was assembled, not as a data resource in its own right.
The 'temporal' field is a rough guess based on file modification context (the folder was retrieved alongside the 2025 ReefNet release); the actual repository commit history was not inspected.
sources.csv contains at least one Red-Sea-related entry by pattern match, but the full list of CoralNet sources actually scraped for the Red Sea portion of ReefNet was not enumerated.
